findings.title: List silently omits bad run records
findings.detail: List calls Load for each persisted run record and continues on any error. That makes corrupt, truncated, unreadable, or future-schema records indistinguishable from absent records to recovery callers. Because this registry is the durable bead-run adoption source after daemon death, silent omission can cause startup to miss a surviving or dead run and then leave a bead stuck or reconcile it through the wrong path.
findings.suggested_fix: Only ignore expected non-record artifacts and TOCTOU disappearance. Return an error, or return records plus per-record errors/stubs, for corrupt/unreadable .json records so adoption/orphan-sweep code can fail loud or treat them as protected/unknown instead of absent.

findings.title: Atomic write is not durable across filesystem crashes
findings.detail: Write uses os.WriteFile followed by os.Rename, but never fsyncs the temp file before rename or fsyncs the parent directory after rename. The function can return success while the run registry entry or renamed directory entry is still only in volatile cache. For a registry whose purpose is crash/restart adoption, a power loss or filesystem crash in that window can lose the record and make an in-flight run invisible on restart.
findings.suggested_fix: Mirror the project’s durable atomic-write pattern: create a unique temp file, write all bytes, fsync the file, close it, rename to the target, then fsync the parent directory. Clean up the temp file on all failures.

findings.title: RunID is used as a path component without validation
findings.detail: recordPath joins runID directly into .harmonik/runs/<runID>.json. The package comment assumes RunIDs are UUIDs, but the exported Write, Load, and Remove APIs do not enforce that. A bad caller or corrupted value containing path separators or '..' can address files outside the runs directory, and even accidental malformed IDs can create records that List later ignores or mishandles.
findings.suggested_fix: Validate runID at every exported API boundary before constructing paths. Accept only the canonical RunID/UUID shape, reject path separators and traversal components, and consider deriving the file name from a typed core.RunID instead of a raw string.
